title: Goal Contracts and Observable Completion · Failure Case: Goal Hierarchy
summary: Turn a vague long-running objective into an executable goal contract. Planned artifact: Add and verify the loops goal hierarchy increment in a measurable goal and done-condition specification. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.
Revise goals explicitly when new evidence changes scope or feasibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Build Goal Hierarchy evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: loops-goal-hierarchy-1edall" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Goal Hierarchy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: loops-goal-hierarchy-1edall
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Goal Hierarchy, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Defend the Goal Hierarchy model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal Hierarchy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.
